name: two distinct owned items collide on the owns identity key
description: NEW GAP (distinct from the 4 known) and the headline ownership failure. The structural identity key is (subject, entity, predicate, qualifier) -- pipeline._existing_facts filters on exactly those four and NOT on object_entity_id. So owning a Civic and a kayak, both emitted as state-kind Me.owns with an empty qualifier, share one identity key. The second insert (kayak) sees the Civic as the current head of Me.owns and, because state is newest-wins, SUPERSEDES it (SCD-2 close) and files a spurious fact_conflict -- the system now believes I traded my Civic for a kayak. Two unrelated possessions wrongly form one revision chain. Spec-correct (docs/ANALYSIS.md 'property graph': me.owns -> [thing] is keyed by the thing; distinct things are distinct edges): both edges stay active, no chain, no review. xfail until the candidate read or identity key disambiguates by object (own_many_items_qualifier_disambiguates shows the qualifier workaround that DOES pass today). Probe both notes use qualifier='' to mimic a model that did not coin a per-item qualifier.
xfail: owns predicate collision: identity key (subject,entity,predicate,qualifier) ignores object_entity_id, so a second distinct owned item supersedes the first (pipeline._existing_facts + supersession.decide state branch)
